Hey guys, JB in the house. I'm back on the beach for daily motivations and skills training. So we finished, I can't believe we finished our 12 week cycle for straight line certification. So what does that mean? Well, that means that the first 12 weeks when someone signs up for the certification program, those daily motivations and weekly intensives are strategically positioned so they correspond to the content that you're learning in certification. That's just going on there. So, you know, let's say from week one, you're going through cracking the code, so my daily motivations reflect that, you get it? But now you've been through this, and then we'll need to go on then. Two would be unconscious communication through, it'd be state management, you get it? So what I'm doing is I'm motivating you and giving you skills reinforcement training on things that you're actually learning at that moment. All right, but now that you're in, I guess, week 13, right, what are we on about, right? And I jumped the habit. Either way, here we are, and I got a blank slate record, so anything I want, which is pretty damn awesome, right? So here's what I'm thinking about. I was thinking about the idea of how I came up with this system of daily motivations. Because what was it about daily motivating people that made it so crucially such a linchpin to this program? And I remember that it was like, 1988, right? And I attended someone else's event. So there was a guy named Stu Traves, who's an old Wall Street warthog, right? He was sort of a sales trainer, right? And I heard this guy get up and speaking for like, look, there's 50 people in the room, right? And I had a feeling that kind of motivated afterwards. You know, I remember saying, wow, I felt kind of like I wanted to jump and pick up the phone. I said, you know what? I said, I should make sure. I remember the exact moment that I need to make sure that every single time my guys are picking up that phone every morning, day in, day out, I have to make sure they're in that right state, that positive state of mind. And that was the first time I said to myself, you know what? I can't leave this up to chance. And that's what it's really all about. It's about leaving it up to chance. Without being pushed, like outside intervention, there's two ways for this, guys. Either you show up by accident, hoping you're in the right state of mind. In other words, you hope that things are lining up in your life right now. You're in that mindset where you're like, bam, it feels great. The word is filling. Or you can ensure that result by actually making it happen yourself. You make it happen. The reason I'm in your ear, guys, every single morning is to act as an insurance policy on your results. You know, sometimes, yeah, I'll say something, I'll make a breakthrough. And there's been some moments, like I said, I've been doing looping action. There was one time I did a looping training like this. And I just said something. I was like, wow, it was a really great breakthrough that even I made, you know, that I never really discussed looping in that sort of way. And it was almost like, bam, that was like a breakthrough moment. But by and large, what these daily motivations are about is about making sure that you attack each day at the top of your game. You get it? That knows, without outside intervention, you got like a 20% shot at most of you showing up at the top of your game. And to me, those are really crappy odds. I don't know why you'd want to go through life with a one in five shot to be your best self. So I'm in your ear. These motivations are so crucial because I'm in your ear, why are you driving you to success each day, ensuring that you don't get caught up in these negative self-reinforcing patterns? What do I mean by that? A negative self-reinforcing pattern is when you start doing, when things are going the wrong way and you're on a negative role, you're devolving into a slump. And each day, like the results they had before, and have not been good, and it starts feeding on itself and feeding on itself and feeding on itself. And without any outside intervention, that slump could go on for three, four, five months. Seriously, sales could slump for a very long time. Why? Because what happens is that self-talk that you make, your own inner dialogue, it becomes really toxic. And remember from the training certification, when you're looking for red, you're going to find red. So if you're looking for negative things, you're looking to be the glass half empty sort of person, right? You're going to find all the reasons why the glass is indeed half empty. Conversely, if you're a glass half full sort of guy, right? Or you get yourself in that mindset, you're just noticing things that make the glass feel or appear to be half full. I'm here every morning, five days a week. It almost like how we get a little short, no weekends, right? I'm here to ensure that you don't get caught up in these negative patterns. Because once you do, you're screwed. It's the most destructive thing to your success. It's to literally allow yourself to succumb to this negative loop where yesterday's dismal performance sets the stage for tomorrow's even more dismal performance and more dismal performance. So on and so forth. Until literally, you're at that point in no return, where you're just like, you walk into the day almost knowing you're going to fail. We cannot allow that to happen. And guys, guess what? It's so easy to protect against. It's simply a matter of bam. Listening to this motivation, opening yourself up to the idea that, you know what? I can have someone in my life who actually is my mentor, who I trust, who I'm going to allow to coach me. You're letting me coach you. Let me help you. I'm going to tell you what, help me help you. Seriously. You're letting me intervene. You do not want to be your own coach and your own ball game. You don't. Very hard to coach yourself guys. I'm a great coach. I'm probably the best coach in the world, but I wouldn't coach myself. I don't coach myself. I want inspiration. I am my go-to people as well. It doesn't matter who they are. The point is, is you can't coach yourself. You have to have someone from the outside come in and essentially, you know, because it's so weird, but like, you know, it's, you know, can't see the forest through the trees. That's exactly what happens. You know what? I have these, actually I have one coming up in October and I think it'll be three weeks. Another mastermind. Once a, not twice a year, once a quarter, I do. Actually three times a year, right? And invariably, you know, they're always very wealthy, very successful people. And what happens is, is that invariably one of them will always be in a position where they think there's no way out, they think it's terrible. And yet the other people in the room are like, what are you talking about guys? It's an easy pathway out. You start as, when you get caught up in a negative of your own life, it's almost like there's no options. You can't see any way out. You feel like you're in a box and then there's just no, no shop. And someone with a fresh set of eyes who's not saddled with the same negative emotional bag as you are, looks at your life as, oh my God, you're not, it was just happening the other day. I had a guy, a friend of mine from New Zealand, right? He calls me up, he's telling me about, you know, things are so terrible and they're like, oh, he's having a little setback, right? And I'm like, buddy, on the scale of terrible, with, you know, 10 being the most terrible thing in the world and one being not that terrible. You like that, like, there'd be a four. You know, it's not the most terrible thing in the world. And yet in his own life, he essentially made this thing, he'd conjured it up, he'd twisted it around by the time he was done, he'd taken, you know, it was just the basic challenge in life and business and turned it into this impossible outcome. All right? So that's what these daily motivations are about. They're about me intervening, to show up, to make sure that you show up at the top of the game every day. Remember, whatever skills you possess right now, you possess them. You have what you have, right? It's your ability to bring your best self to the table. That's the difference in success and failure at this level when you don't really make progress. It's bringing your best self to the table. So let's rock and roll this week, guys. Let's kick some serious butt. I love you all. We'll talk again tomorrow. Take care.
